title: Chewy Marketing Strategy: How the Pet Retailer Redefined Growth
text: Chewy's marketing strategy has evolved from heavy customer acquisition spending in early years to efficient, profitable growth through digital and CRM investments. The company has successfully leveraged multiple channels including direct traffic, search engine optimization, app-based marketing, and influencer partnerships. Early pandemic growth drove customer acquisition at scale, but post-pandemic strategy focused on retention and monetization through higher spending per customer. Chewy's ability to improve marketing efficiency—evidenced by declining customer acquisition costs while growing customers—demonstrates sophisticated digital marketing capabilities. The mobile app has become a key marketing channel, with higher engagement, order frequency, and retention rates compared to web-based customers. Chewy+ membership platform integrates loyalty and marketing functions, creating personalized engagement opportunities. The shift from acquiring customers at any cost to profitable customer lifetime value optimization has been a key driver of margin expansion. Sponsored ads platform provides additional marketing leverage, creating vendor relationships and high-margin revenue while improving product discovery for customers.
